title: Dryopteris grossa
taxonomy: Dryopteris grossa is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of evergreen fern native to East Asia. The genus Dryopteris is a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns or shield ferns. The specific epithet 'grossa' is a Latin word meaning 'thick' or 'coarse', likely referring to the texture of the fronds. This species is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes various types of ferns.

intro: Dryopteris grossa, also known as the Japanese shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the genus Dryopteris, characterized by its large, leathery fronds and dark green color. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris grossa include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often processed into teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the preparation of tinctures and powders, to treat a range of health conditions.
